exports.docs: A basic HTTP/1.1 client, mapped on top a single TCP/TLS connection. Pipelining is disabled by default.
`true` after `client.close()` has been called.
`true` after `client.destroyed()` has been called or `client.close()` has been called and the client shutdown has completed.
Property to get and set the pipelining factor.
Aggregate stats for a Client.

Convert a MIMEType object to a string.
@see https://mimesniff.spec.whatwg.org/#serialize-a-mime-type
Provides a way to easily construct a set of key/value pairs representing form fields and their values, which can then be easily sent using fetch().
An alias for FormData#entries()

Deletes a key and its value(s) from a `FormData` object.

@param name The name of the key you want to delete.

Returns an [`iterator`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Iteration_protocols) allowing to go through the `FormData` key/value pairs.
The key of each pair is a string; the value is a [`FormDataValue`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/FormDataEntryValue).
Executes given callback function for each field of the FormData instance

Returns an [`iterator`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Iteration_protocols) allowing to go through all keys contained in this `FormData` object.
Each key is a `string`.

Returns an [`iterator`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Iteration_protocols) allowing to go through all values contained in this object `FormData` object.
Each value is a [`FormDataValue`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/FormDataEntryValue).
A basic H2C client, mapped on top a single TCP connection. Pipelining is disabled by default.
`true` after `client.close()` has been called.
`true` after `client.destroyed()` has been called or `client.close()` has been called and the client shutdown has completed.
Property to get and set the pipelining factor.
The header type declaration of `undici`.
The header type declaration of `undici` for outgoing requests.

symbols.docs:
Normalize spacing in a title.

Trim leading and trailing ASCII whitespace and replace each non-empty run of
ASCII whitespace characters with one literal space. Return an empty string
when the input is empty or contains only ASCII whitespace.

@example normalizeSpacing("  Jaunt\tTS  ") // => "Jaunt TS"
symbols.name: normalizeSpacing
